title: forestiera segregata var. pinetorum
intro: Forestiera segregata var. pinetorum, also known as Mexican princewood or pineland boxwood, is a variety of shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Forestiera segregata var. pinetorum typically involve drying and infusing the leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
